title: 403 Forbidden Error on Twitter Media Upload
symptom: When attempting to upload an image to Twitter using the HTTP Request Node, a 403 Forbidden error is returned.
not_this_if: Using a different node or integration
root_cause: The 403 Forbidden error indicates that the request is not authorized. This can happen if the OAuth2 credentials are not set up correctly or if the required permissions are not granted for the media upload.
fix_steps: Stop using the predefined X credential in n8n.
Create a new standard 'OAuth2 API' credential for the Twitter node.
In the scope field, manually type 'tweet.read tweet.write users.read media.write offline.access'.
Save the credential and reconnect your Twitter account to generate a fresh token.
agent_instruction: Create a new OAuth2 API credential for Twitter with the correct authorization and token URLs.
